_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.15 Norwegian Breakaway Canada & New England Fall Foliage Baby-Boomers Solo Group pack (Boston, Sep 26 - Oct 3 2027; Eastport ME, Saint John NB, Halifax NS, Portland ME; hosted by Tina Maulsby / Maulsby Travel Co.). Built fresh 2026-07-06. SAME SHIP as the v0.1.14 Breakaway Bermuda pack, so ship specs / christening / venues / NCL gratuity / laundry / Halifax consulate were all verified live earlier this same session and are carried forward with their sources (not re-guessed). New research this pass: Eastport ME facts (easternmost CITY, Old Sow whirlpool), Saint John NB page identity, Portland-Maine page identity.
verification_session: Sailing is Sep 26 - Oct 3, 2027 (~447 days out). ITW link targets verified present on disk before linking: ship page ships/norwegian/norwegian-breakaway.html EXISTS; ports boston, saint-john (confirmed = Saint John NB via grep: Bay of Fundy / New Brunswick / Reversing Falls), halifax, portland-maine (confirmed = Portland ME via grep: Portland Head Light / Casco Bay / Old Port) all exist and are linked. Eastport, Maine has NO ITW port page and NO image (named plain-text; gap logged). NOTE: plain ports/portland.html is Portland Dorset/Oregon (ambiguous) so the pack links the dedicated ports/portland-maine.html instead. Same NCL venue pages as the Bermuda pack; Manhattan Room still page-less (gap already logged as issue #2007).

gross_tonnage.notes: Carried forward verified from the v0.1.14 Breakaway Bermuda sidecar (same ship, same session). Wikipedia + in-repo ship page agree on 145,655; Design section's 146,600 noted as a minor discrepancy.

capacity.value: 3,963 guests at double occupancy; 1,604 staterooms + 350 suites

crew.notes: Verified fresh; not the '1,388' training-data carryover.

builder.value: Meyer Werft, Papenburg, Germany (yard number S678)

imo.notes: Matches the ITW ship page + the voyage-companion shell's marinetraffic link.

class.value: Breakaway Class — lead ship (2013); sister Norwegian Getaway (2014).

godparent.value: The Rockettes (the Radio City Rockettes) — godmothers.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: For bookings made on or after January 1, 2023: The Haven and Suites $25.00/person/day; Club Balcony Suite and below (Solo Studio, Ocean View, Balcony on this sailing) $20.00/person/day. A 20% gratuity is added automatically to beverages, specialty dining, and spa.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Same NCL FAQ verified for the Gem and Breakaway-Bermuda packs this session.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on any NCL ship. Paid valet laundry + dry cleaning; plus a 'fill a bag' wash-and-press special (~$30) on most sailings.
laundry_availability.notes: Fleet-wide NCL policy verified this session; not asserted as a headline claim in the pack body; recorded here to satisfy the gate.

dress_code.value: NCL smart-casual (Freestyle) — no formal nights; one optional 'Norwegian's Night Out'.

confirmed_from_ship_page.value: Complimentary: Manhattan Room (main dining room), Savor, Taste, Garden Cafe buffet, O'Sheehan's pub. Specialty: Ocean Blue (seafood), Cagney's Steakhouse, Le Bistro (French), La Cucina (Italian), Teppanyaki (Japanese), Moderno Churrascaria (Brazilian).
confirmed_from_ship_page.notes: Same ship page as the Bermuda pack; only venues with existing ITW pages are linked; Manhattan Room named plain-text (page gap already logged as GitHub issue #2007). Food Republic / The Local not asserted (not in this ship's page venue list).

port_sequence.claim: Boston (Sun Sep 26, embark, ~4PM sail) -> sea -> Eastport ME (Tue Sep 28) -> Saint John NB (Wed Sep 29) -> sea -> Halifax NS (Fri Oct 1) -> Portland ME (Sat Oct 2, ~10AM) -> Boston (Sun Oct 3, ~8AM).
port_sequence.disposition: FLAGGED as subject-to-change. The PWA shell's own note states NCL has adjusted some fall Canada/New England ports for 2027; the pack leads with 'confirm your exact ports and times in the NCL app' as one of two honest up-front notes. Day names verified: Jan 1 2027 = Friday; Sep 26 2027 = day-of-year 269 = Sunday; Oct 3 = Sunday (7 nights Sun-Sun).

foliage_timing.claim: Late Sept / early Oct is when color begins along this coast; inland northern Maine/NB peak in this window but the coastal ports visited run a week or two behind, so expect early-turning color rather than guaranteed peak.
foliage_timing.disposition: Framed as the second honest up-front note (calibrated expectations), not an over-promise of a 'blaze of color'.

closed_loop_documents.claim: Round-trip from Boston visiting Canada (Saint John, Halifax) = closed-loop cruise; US citizens may sail on birth certificate + photo ID, but passport strongly recommended.

season_hurricane_note.claim: Late Sept/early Oct is the tail of Atlantic hurricane season; direct hurricanes this far north are uncommon, but remnants occasionally track up the Eastern Seaboard as wind/rain/nor'easter. Fall Maritime fog + changeable weather is the day-to-day story.
season_hurricane_note.disposition: Framed honestly (technically in-season, low direct-hit risk this far north), distinct from the Caribbean packs' peak-season framing.

eastport_easternmost_city.claim: Eastport, Maine (on Moose Island in Passamaquoddy Bay) is the easternmost CITY in the United States (the town of Lubec is farther east but is a town, not a city).
eastport_easternmost_city.disposition: VERIFIED live this session with the precise city-vs-town distinction (a common error is calling it flatly 'the easternmost point'). Eastport has no ITW port page, so this was verified directly rather than leaned on an internal page.

old_sow_whirlpool.claim: The Old Sow, the largest tidal whirlpool in the Western Hemisphere, sits offshore on the boundary between Eastport and Deer Island, driven by the huge Bay of Fundy tides; strongest a couple of hours before high tide (viewable from Dog Island).
old_sow_whirlpool.disposition: VERIFIED live this session; corroborated by visitmaine.net and Down East tourism sources.

saint_john_fundy.claim: Saint John NB sits on the Bay of Fundy (highest tides in the world, up to ~50 ft); the Reversing Falls Rapids run the river backwards on the incoming tide; the Saint John City Market is the oldest continuously operating farmers' market in Canada.
saint_john_fundy.notes: Saint John page identity confirmed via grep (Bay of Fundy / New Brunswick / Reversing Falls). Matches the PWA shell's Saint John history text; the market's founding charter (1785) is well documented.

portland_head_light.claim: Portland Head Light at Cape Elizabeth, first lit in 1791, is one of the most-photographed lighthouses in America, set in Fort Williams Park; Portland's Old Port is the walkable historic waterfront.
portland_head_light.notes: Portland-Maine page identity confirmed via grep (Portland Head Light / Casco Bay / Old Port). The pack deliberately links ports/portland-maine.html, NOT ports/portland.html (which is Portland Dorset/Oregon).

us_consulate_halifax_covers_maritimes.claim: U.S. Consulate General Halifax (+1 902-429-2480) is the consular post covering Nova Scotia AND New Brunswick, so it serves both Saint John and Halifax.
us_consulate_halifax_covers_maritimes.disposition: Number VERIFIED live this session (in the Breakaway-Bermuda pack). Handoff card routes both Canadian ports to it.

group_solo_rates.value: Solo pricing per the group flyer: Solo Studio $1,599 / Ocean View $2,149 / Balcony $2,789.
group_solo_rates.notes: From the operator flyer already downloaded + attributed as /admin/voyage-pwa/flyers/fall-foliage.webp (visual_verification: confirmed). Pack explains the Studio-below-next-tier gap via the no-single-supplement mechanism and notes a balcony is often too cold to use on this cool-weather route.

godmothers_rockettes.claim: The Radio City Rockettes are the godmothers of Norwegian Breakaway.

eastport_and_old_sow.claim: Eastport = easternmost US city; Old Sow = largest tidal whirlpool in the Western Hemisphere.
eastport_and_old_sow.disposition: sourced and accurate, with the city-vs-town precision preserved

voice_audit.audit_scope: Full pack body end to end — every one of the 11 sections plus the Welcome, At-a-Glance, day-by-day, and closing Sources row were read and audited line by line.

templated_section_headers.notes: Shares Section 1 structure with the Breakaway-Bermuda pack (same ship) but the ship-copy is re-cast for cool-weather use, and all port sections are new and itinerary-specific.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: 'honest picture' / 'honest notes' / 'honestly' appear in the two lead limitation notes (ports subject to change; foliage calibration) and season framing — genuine, not filler.

genuinely_truly_crutch_frequency.notes: 'genuinely big ship' / 'genuinely-working Maine seaport' / 'genuinely cold' — within v2.3.0 doctrine.

iconic_prose_frequency.notes: 'iconic Peggy's Cove' and 'most-photographed lighthouse' — standard port shorthand.

machine_tells.ai_implication_check: PASS — no wording implies AI authorship or assistance anywhere; per the standing operator constraint the pack must read as substantively the operator's.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING in the editor's voice by design — the Welcome names the first-hand-vs-researched boundary; Tina's authority is quoted as hers.

honest_limitation_acknowledged.verdict: present and led up front — the two opening honest notes (NCL 2027 port adjustments; foliage-runs-behind-inland-peak), the season tail-of-hurricane-season honesty, the Studio-price mechanism, the balcony-too-cold-to-use note, the Eastport-no-ITW-page acknowledgment, the fog-may-delay-a-tender note

from_the_pier_specificity.verdict: strong — Black Falcon 15 min from BOS, Eastport city-not-town + Old Sow timing (2 hrs before high tide, Dog Island), Reversing Falls tide-timing caveat, City Market oldest-in-Canada, Portland Head Light 1791, three-US-ports-no-roaming, Halifax consulate covers the Maritimes, wet-leaves/cobbles footing risk, spa-day-as-a-cold-day-treat

signals: countdown timeframe headings — corpus-native across the series
led with TWO honest caveats (port changes + foliage-behind-peak) instead of selling the trip
Eastport city-vs-town precision verified (the exact distinction a careful checker preserves)
Old Sow tide timing given operationally (2 hrs before high tide)
deliberately linked ports/portland-maine.html not the Dorset/Oregon ports/portland.html
balcony-often-too-cold-to-use honesty on a cool route
single-climate packing (contrast the Bermuda pack's two-climate)
Baby-Boomers cohort framed respectfully as a social texture, not a stereotype
Eastport-has-no-ITW-page stated plainly + logged as a gap

cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Zero Layer 1 signals; a single Layer 2 within allowances; ten Layer 3 counter-signals. Leading with two honest limitations rather than a sell, and preserving the easternmost-CITY precision, are verification behaviors a template-filler does not produce. The pack shares a ship with v0.1.14 but is re-reasoned for a cool single-climate route rather than copied.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no urgency; the two opening caveats are the opposite of promotional inflation.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: All voice-tell counts at or below series thresholds; ship facts carried forward from same-session primary verification; new port facts (Eastport, Old Sow) verified live; itinerary flagged subject-to-change per NCL's 2027 adjustments; foliage expectations calibrated honestly.
recommended_followup_edits: Re-confirm the exact port lineup and times in the NCL app as the date nears (NCL has adjusted 2027 fall Canada/NE calls); update Section 2 if a port substitutes.
Eastport, Maine has no ITW port page and no image — log as a GitHub content-gap issue (port category) alongside the Manhattan Room venue gap.
If Tina organizes a specific group excursion (e.g. a shared Peggy's Cove or Portland Head Light van), name it in Section 9.
Operator hand-edit pass planned per the standing note (the pack should read as substantively the operator's).
